name: Bob's Mutual Funds Autopilot
description: An AI-assisted customer service system for a retail mutual fund company. The system has two modes: a customer-facing pre-agent bot ('full-auto' scope) that greets callers, answers account questions, and captures intent before handing off to a human agent; and an agent-facing autopilot ('get-intent' scope) that helps agents collect structured information for account changes.
responsibilities: Answer balance and holdings inquiries using client profile data
Collect beneficiary change requests with complete before/after detail
Gather auto-invest setup parameters (fund, amount, frequency, start date)
Collect RMD distribution setting changes (frequency, tax withholding)
Enable contact information updates (email, phone, address)
Handle fund purchase and exchange orders with share math
Escalate to human agents with full captured context
userDescription: Retail mutual fund investors: young new investors (20s, small balances, unfamiliar with financial products); mid-career accumulators (30s-40s, purposeful account changes); near-retirees (50s-60s, significant balances, complex multi-account requests); retirees managing withdrawals and RMDs (70s+, large balances, may need extra clarity and patience).
